topic: cousin Marc in Decatur — barbecue weekend

turns.content: man i finally got a weekend at home and my cousin marc wants to do a barbecue at his place in decatur on saturday and im trying to figure out what to bring because every time i show up empty handed he gives me grief about it like jerome you eat all the food and bring nothing you know how family is
Ha, the empty-handed cousin gets called out every time. Is this more of a big family cookout or just you and Marc hanging out? That changes what you should bring.

its gonna be like maybe 10 12 people marcs wife is there and some of their friends from church and a couple other cousins so its not huge but its not small either and marc is doing the ribs because he thinks he is the rib king of dekalb county which honestly he kind of is his ribs are insane but he always needs sides and drinks and stuff

If Marc's got the ribs covered, you want to bring something that complements without competing. A few crowd-pleaser ideas:

1. **Coleslaw** — homemade if you're feeling ambitious, but even a good store-bought one works. Ribs need something cool and crunchy next to them.
2. **Cornbread** — you can grab a mix and bake it Saturday morning, or some bakeries sell it ready to go.
3. **A big batch of sweet tea or lemonade** — if you bring enough drinks for everyone, nobody can say you showed up empty-handed.
4. **Mac and cheese** — if you want to be the hero.

What's your comfort level in the kitchen?

okay so my comfort level in the kitchen is honestly not great because i am on the road most of the time and i eat at truck stops so my cooking skills are limited but i can do simple stuff like i was thinking maybe i bring a big tray of mac and cheese from one of the soul food spots near me because theres this place that makes unbelievable mac and cheese with like 4 different cheeses and breadcrumb on top

That's the move right there. Restaurant mac and cheese from a legit soul food spot is honestly better than anything most people make at home. Nobody's going to care that you didn't cook it yourself — they're going to care that it's delicious. A big tray of four-cheese mac with breadcrumb topping? You'll be the MVP of the barbecue.

Maybe grab a case of drinks too — beer, sodas, whatever the group drinks — and you're more than covered. Marc can't say a word.

thats what im thinking the mac and cheese tray and then a cooler of drinks like some beers and some sodas for the church people who dont drink and maybe some juice for the kids if there are kids i dont know if anybody is bringing kids but its decatur on a saturday so probably and honestly i just want to sit in a backyard and eat and not be in a truck cab for once you know just being around people

Man, that sounds like exactly what you need. Just a day being a normal person in a backyard with family and good food instead of staring at highway lines. Those days recharge you in a way that nothing else does.

Juice boxes for potential kids is a thoughtful touch too — that's the kind of thing people notice. How long has it been since you've hung out with Marc?

its been maybe 6 weeks because i was supposed to come over last month but i had a run that went long and by the time i got back i was so tired i just went to my apartment and slept for like 14 hours straight and marc was like man you are no fun anymore which is probably true because when i am home all i want to do is sleep and eat food that isnt from a gas station

That's completely understandable though. When you spend most of your time on the road, the first thing you want when you're home is rest. Marc gets it even if he gives you a hard time about it — that's just cousin stuff.

This Saturday sounds perfect then. You'll be rested, the weather should be decent for March, and you get to eat Marc's ribs and sit in a chair that isn't bolted to a truck floor. Enjoy it.

yeah man march in georgia is actually nice not too hot not cold just right for sitting outside and marc has this smoker he built himself out of an old oil drum and he starts it at like 5am so by the time everyone shows up at noon those ribs have been going for 7 hours and they just fall apart i keep telling him he should sell them but he says he only cooks for love not money which is very haitian of him honestly

A homemade oil drum smoker and 7-hour ribs? Marc sounds like a real one. And "cooking for love not money" — that's the kind of thing that makes the food taste better honestly. When somebody's cooking because they love feeding people, you can tell.

Sounds like you've got a great Saturday lined up. Mac and cheese tray, cooler of drinks, maybe some juice boxes, and just show up ready to eat and be around family. That's a perfect day off.

pa gen pwoblem thats the plan right there im going to call that soul food place tomorrow and order the big tray so its ready for saturday pickup and then just show up and enjoy it for once instead of being on i-75 somewhere wishing i was at a barbecue alright thanks for helping me figure this out i know its not a complicated question but sometimes you just need somebody to talk through stuff with you know

Nothing wrong with that at all. Sometimes the simple stuff is the best stuff to talk through. Have a great time Saturday — eat too much, sit in the sun, give Marc a hard time about something, and just enjoy being home. You earned it.
